sections.paragraph:
θροέω 1 aor. pass. ptc. θροηθείς (Trag. et al., in act. and mid. w. the mng.: ‘cry out, tell out, speak, announce’, etc.—act.: Jos., Ant. 18, 234; 19, 137) in the NT only pass. in the sense be inwardly aroused (cp. Tetrast. Iamb. 2, 1, 4 p. 286; Malalas 41, 12; SSol 5:4), be disturbed or frightened (TestAbr B 111, 7 [Stone p. 70]) 2 Th 2:2. μὴ θροεῖσθε Mt 24:6; Mk 13:7; AcPl Ha 5, 25. θροηθέντες καὶ ἔμφοβοι γενόμενοι Lk 24:37 v.l.—DELG s.v. θρέομαι.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

act. act. = active

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

NT NT = New Testament

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Malalas Malalas = John Malalas, Chronographia VI a.d.—List 5

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 460
